data.description:
## Why
One plan defect currently mints up to N blocking findings via multi-criterion co-citation (ticket a879: two nonexistent symbols x five criteria = 10 blocking findings per round for 3 consecutive rounds; ticket c9d8 round 1: one underspecified escape-hatch surfaced as 4 blocking findings). Agents face an inflated block wall for a single fix-unit, and per-criterion block tallies are distorted for future calibration. Dedup must GROUP, never drop: the gate's strength is unchanged.

## What
- After verdict production and before the sidecar emit (the seam in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py where the rising/completion floors already run), group blocking findings that share a fix-unit: same normalized location AND same core claim. Grouping key: reuse the existing reword-tolerant norm_id machinery in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py where it already matches across criteria; where norm_id is criterion-scoped, derive the fix-unit key from (location, claim-normalization) - the implementation must state which in an inline design note.
- Surface ONE primary blocking finding per group (highest priority), with its co-criteria and the folded findings listed on the primary as members[]; folded findings keep decision=block in the sidecar (calibration data lossless) but are not separately rendered.
- CLI renders the primary with a "(+N co-criteria: E4, G1G2, ...)" suffix.
- Keep the change out of src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py (epic/audit-enrichment touches it); this is a pipeline-layer grouping, not a decision change.

## Scope
IN: grouping of BLOCKING findings only, sidecar-lossless, CLI rendering, tests. OUT: advisory grouping (the advisory cap already bounds volume), cross-round dedup (that is the rising floor's job), any threshold/posture change.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A synthetic verdict with 5 blocking findings citing the same location and claim across 5 criteria surfaces exactly 1 primary blocking finding with 4 members; the verdict remains BLOCK. Oracle: new unit test; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "dedup or fanout" -x` green.
- [ ] Findings at different locations or with different claims are NOT grouped. Oracle: negative-case unit test in the same module.
- [ ] The sidecar retains all 5 findings with decision=block plus the group key (lossless for calibration replay). Oracle: unit test reads the emitted sidecar dict and asserts 5 block-decision findings and a shared group id.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all pass.
data.title: Cross-criterion fix-unit grouping for blocking findings (one defect, one block group)
